title: Terminus Station
description: An isolated space station. A distress signal. A crew of three, and something that survived the void.
author: Terminal Stories

scenes.speaker: commander
text: SHIP'S LOG, COMMANDER A. REYES, DAY 1
Docking successful at Terminus-7 Station. No response to hails for 72 hours.
The station drifts slightly out of its normal orbit. Systems partially online.
Dax and Vasiliev await my order in the airlock.

choices.action: "Dax, open that airlock. Vasiliev, stay behind me."
choices.consequence: The airlock groans open. The station air smells of scorched metal and something else, organic.

choices.action: "Dax, get me the bio-signature readings first."
choices.consequence: Dax frowns. "Commander... there are signatures. But they're moving strangely."

text: Dax pulls up the data on his pad. His hands don't shake, but they should.

text.speaker: engineer
text.text: "Twenty-two human signatures originally logged. I'm reading... eleven. Stable."

text.speaker: engineer
text.text: "And one more. Nothing in the database matches it."

choices.action: "Weapons out. Delta formation. We go in."
choices.consequence: The weapons snap out of their holsters. You enter the station.

choices.action: "Give me their exact positions. We find them first."
choices.consequence: "Section B, level 3. All grouped in one place."

text: The emergency lights flash red. Half the panels have been torn off.
On the wall, someone has scrawled in jagged letters: 'DON'T LOOK FOR TANNER'.
Dr. Vasiliev crouches beside a dark stain on the floor.

choices.text: Investigate the message on the wall
choices.action: You examine the letters closely. They were gouged in with something hard.
choices.consequence: Behind the torn panel, you find an audio log.

choices.action: "We don't have time. Survivors first."
choices.consequence: Vasiliev follows without a word. Dax throws a last glance at the message.

choices.action: "Dax, Tanner, check the crew registry."
choices.consequence: "Tanner, Marcus. Chief biologist. Went missing 6 days ago."

text: [RECORDING DAY 4 SINCE THE INCIDENT]
A panicked voice, barely recognizable:
"He changed. Tanner changed. It's not Tanner anymore. It wears his face but it doesn't breathe the same way..."
"It has access to every system. It knows where we are. Why is it waiting?"
[END OF RECORDING]

choices.action: "Section B. Fast."
choices.consequence: You run through the corridors. Something moves in the ducts above you.

choices.action: "If we want to understand what we're up against, we need his research."
choices.consequence: Dax hesitates. "That's the opposite direction from the survivors, Commander."

text: The biology lab is in a catastrophic state. Containers shattered. Data wiped.
Except one screen, still lit. A file open: 'ZETA-ORGANISM, FINAL REPORT'.
You skim it: '...perfect mimicry... memory absorbed... detection near-impossible...'
Someone sighs behind you. You turn slowly.

choices.action: You look the engineer in the eyes. Something is off.
choices.consequence: "Of course, Commander. I'm still here." His voice is slightly off. Slightly.

choices.action: You spin around, weapon raised.
choices.consequence: Dax throws up his hands. "Commander! It's me!" You study his face. Do you believe him?

text: Eleven people. Barricaded in the Section B rec room.
Their faces when you walk in: relief, then... something else.
A woman steps forward. Chief mechanic, according to her badge. Her eyes are dry.
"You came for us. Good. But one of you is going to have to stay."

choices.action: You step toward her, hand on your holster.
choices.consequence: "Because it hunts body heat. Fewer people aboard, fewer trails."

choices.action: "We leave no one behind. Formation, move."
choices.consequence: In the corridor, the ducts open above you.

text: You watch Dax. He's... normal. His movements, the way he talks.
But you read the report. Perfect mimicry. Memory absorbed.
He looks at you with what seems like concern.
Vasiliev edges closer. He murmurs:

choices.action: You keep an eye on Dax while Vasiliev explains.
choices.consequence: "Body temperature. The organism is ectothermic. Dax, hold out your hand."

choices.action: "Forget it. We have a job to do."
choices.consequence: Dax looks relieved. Vasiliev says nothing. You move out.

text: Dax holds out his hand. Vasiliev sets his scanner against it.
A beep. A silence.
Vasiliev looks down.
They look at each other. You're standing between them.

choices.action: "We're a team. We stay a team. End of discussion."
choices.consequence: A moment of tension. Then Dax nods. So does Vasiliev.

choices.text: Isolate them both as a precaution
choices.action: "You both stay behind me. No one touches anyone until we evacuate."
choices.consequence: They agree. One of them, perhaps, is relieved for the wrong reason.

text: The plan: cut the heating systems to throw off the thermal tracker.
The problem: it takes 20 minutes to do from the engine room.

text.speaker: engineer
text.text: "I can do it," Dax says. "But someone needs to cover the corridor."

choices.text: Send Dax to the engine room
choices.action: "Go. Vasiliev and I hold the corridor."
choices.consequence: Dax takes off running. The minutes pass. The ducts groan.

choices.action: "I'll handle it. Dax, you stay with the civilians."
choices.consequence: You run alone through the corridors. Something follows at a distance.

text: The run for the airlocks is chaos.
The ducts burst open above you. Something, BIG, drops into the corridor.
Ahead of you: the airlock. Behind you: your formation scattering in panic.
You can save everyone, or make sure nothing else boards your ship.

choices.text: Hold the airlock open for everyone
choices.action: You hold the airlock open, screaming out names.
choices.consequence: Nine people make it through. Two don't come. Something else is closing in.

choices.text: Seal the airlock and launch immediately
choices.action: "I'm sorry." You seal the airlock.
choices.consequence: The ship launches. Through the porthole, you see the station. Then darkness.

text: 17 minutes. The ducts groan rhythmically.
Vasiliev leans toward you.

text.speaker: doctor
text.text: "There's something I didn't tell you, Commander."

text.speaker: doctor
text.text: "Tanner... I was with him when it happened. I saw what it does. It isn't quick."

choices.action: You keep your eyes on the corridor while you listen.
choices.consequence: He talks. And when he's done, you understand why he's seemed so calm from the start.

choices.action: You cut him off. Eyes on the corridor.
choices.consequence: He goes quiet. And when Dax comes back, you catch something in the doctor's eyes.

text.speaker: doctor
text.text: "I'm immune. My blood chemistry, the organism can't imitate me."

text.speaker: doctor
text.text: "But that doesn't stop me from being a... carrier."

text: He rolls up his sleeve. On his forearm, a web of dark filaments runs beneath the skin.

text.speaker: doctor
text.text: "It happened two days ago. I didn't tell anyone because... I wanted to get out of here."

choices.action: It's the hardest decision you've ever made.
choices.consequence: He nods. "I know. Go get Dax."

choices.text: "You're coming with us. We'll find a treatment."
choices.action: "I don't leave anyone behind."
choices.consequence: He looks at you for a long moment. "You didn't understand what I said, Commander."

text: The engine room is half in darkness. The turbines idle low.
You locate the thermal control panel. Your fingers find the controls.
Behind you, breathing. Steady. Too steady.

choices.text: Cut the systems and face what's behind you
choices.action: You cut the heating with one hand. You draw with the other.
choices.consequence: The lights dim. In the dark, two eyes glowing with a biological light.

choices.text: Cut the systems and run without looking back
choices.action: You shut it all down and run.
choices.consequence: Something follows you down the corridor. Fast. Very fast.

text: That face. You know it.
Marcus Tanner. Just as he was in his file photo. Smiling.
His voice is perfect. His smile is perfect. His eyes don't blink.

choices.action: You fire. Three times.
choices.consequence: The thing drops. Then rises again. "That hurts. But not enough."

choices.action: You keep your weapon trained on it but you don't fire.
choices.consequence: "I am what he knew. What he wanted." A pause. "I can let you go."

scenes.speaker: unknown
text: "A deal. You leave. I stay."
"In exchange... one thing. Take me into your memory. A single memory. Just one."
"I won't imitate you. I only want to... know what it's like to be you."

choices.action: "One memory. That's all."
choices.consequence: Something passes. A brief, cold contact. Then the creature fades into the shadows.

choices.action: "I don't negotiate with something that killed my crew."
choices.consequence: It vanishes into the ducts. You run for the airlocks.

text: The cold sets in fast. The station goes silent in a different way.
You gather the survivors. Dax keeps the count. Eleven. Still eleven.
The trek to the airlocks passes in absolute silence. As if the station were holding its breath.
Your ship is waiting.

choices.action: "Everyone aboard. We launch in three minutes."
choices.consequence: People file on. You count heads. Eleven plus your team. But...

text: The ship pulls away. Terminus-7 Station shrinks in the porthole.
Eleven survivors. Plus you. Plus Dax.
Vasiliev isn't aboard. You don't know whether it was his choice to stay.
The report you'll write will have gaps. Gray areas.
Some truths are too heavy for official forms.
— ENDING: What We Leave Behind —
You watch the station recede.
In the report: 'No survivors. Cause undetermined.'
You know what you did. You know why you did it.
You don't yet know whether you could have done otherwise.
Some nights in space are longer than others.
— ENDING: The Weight of Command —
The ship launches. The station disappears.
In your memory, something has changed. A memory, of childhood, or of something else, feels different.
Not erased. Just... shared.
Somewhere in the vastness of the void, a consciousness that was not human
learns for the first time what it feels like to be afraid, and to act anyway.
— ENDING: An Exchange Among the Stars —
Three days after the return, the first crew member falls ill.
Then a second.
The quarantine protocol takes effect too late.
In your final report, a single line:
'I knew. I chose not to act.'
Sometimes, compassion and responsibility don't point the same way.
— ENDING: The Best of Intentions —
